title: Xtra Buttery Indian Flour Flatbread
description: A buttery and snappy Indian flatbread made with strong white flour, perfect for crowds, this dish is ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly and delicious, featuring a rich and savory flavor profile.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add the melted ghee or butter and lukewarm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or tava over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions and roll each portion into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 6-7 inches in diameter.
Place a flatbread in the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, until it starts to puff up and the edges start to curl.
Brush the flatbread with butter and flip it over, cooking for another 1-2 minutes, until it is golden brown and crispy.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough portions.
